name: Return to the Peñas del Masmut – trail running circular (PR-TE 157)
name_original: Vuelta a las Peñas del Masmut – trail running circular (PR-TE 157)
region_intro: Jogging routes around Peñarroya De Tastavins traverse a diverse landscape within the Puertos de Beceite mountain range. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, featuring ravines carved by the Tastavins river, imposing conglomerate monoliths like the Rocas del Masmut, and extensive pine forests. These natural features provide varied running experiences, from riverside paths to more challenging mountain trails with significant elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Peñarroya De Tastavins The most popular jogging route is Return to the Peñas del Masmut – trail running circular (PR-TE 157), a 10.3 miles (16.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route showcases the imposing Rocas del Masmut. Another top favourite among local runners is View of the Masmut Cliffs – Viewpoint of Masmut loop from Peñarroya de Tastavins / Pena-roja de Tastavins, a difficult 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Masmut Cliffs and the surrounding mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Pena Reservoir – Pantano de Pena loop from Matarraña Nautic, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading around the scenic Pena Reservoir, often completed in about 53 minutes. Jogging around Peñarroya De Tastavins is defined by the Puertos de Beceite mountain range, the dramatic Rocas del Masmut, and the winding Tastavins river ravines. The extensive network of trails offers options for moderate to challenging ability levels. The routes in Peñarroya De Tastavins are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Peñarroya De Tastavins's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: There are over 50 dedicated running routes available in the Peñarroya de Tastavins area, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These trails traverse the diverse landscapes of the Puertos de Beceite mountain range.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are 15 moderate routes available. A good option for a moderate run is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena Reservoir – Pantano de Pena loop from Matarraña Nautic&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.2 miles (8.3 km) long and takes about 53 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views around the reservoir.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on the running trails?
A: Jogging routes in Peñarroya de Tastavins offer a rich variety of natural landscapes. You'll experience the majestic Puertos de Beceite mountain range, dramatic ravines carved by the Tastavins river, extensive pine forests, and imposing conglomerate monoliths like the Rocas del Masmut. The area also features the scenic Ulldecona Reservoir and the picturesque Salt de Robert Waterfall.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boixar loop from Colonia Europa&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 30.7-mile (49.4 km) route with over 2,100 meters of elevation gain, typically taking over 7 hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Masmut Cliffs – Peñas del Masmut Trail loop from Peñarroya de Tastavins&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.8 miles (22.2 km) long.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running paths in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: Many trails in the Peñarroya de Tastavins area are multi-use and allow dogs, making it a great option for running with your canine companion. It's always a good idea to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient circular experience. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983207483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Peñas del Masmut – trail running circular (PR-TE 157)&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 10.3-mile (16.5 km) loop that showcases the impressive Rocas del Masmut.

Q: What interesting sights or attractions can I see along the jogging routes?
A: While jogging, you can encounter several natural and cultural highlights. Keep an eye out for the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4623502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fenellasa Rock Paintings&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from riverside paths to challenging mountain trails, and the spectacular views of the Puertos de Beceite mountain range and landmarks like the Rocas del Masmut.

Q: Is there public transport access to the running routes, or is parking available?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, Peñarroya de Tastavins is generally accessible by car. Many routes start from or near the town, where parking facilities are typically available. For routes further afield, it's advisable to check local parking options near the starting points.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the famous Rocas del Masmut?
A: Yes, the Rocas del Masmut are a prominent feature of the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Masmut Cliffs – Viewpoint of Masmut loop from Peñarroya de Tastavins&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.4-mile (10.3 km) path specifically designed to offer panoramic views of these imposing monoliths and the surrounding mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Peñarroya de Tastavins?
A: The region's diverse climate means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winter can be cool, but many trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there any running events or organized activities in the area?
A: Yes, Peñarroya de Tastavins is known for its outdoor activities, including the annual 'Tastavins Trail' event. This event highlights the town's suitability for running and offers various routes adapted for different fitness levels, encouraging participation from individuals and families to enjoy the natural surroundings.
tips: The Masmut Viewpoint is a spectacular spot located near Peñarroya de Tastavins. From this viewpoint, you can admire the impressive Masmut Rocks, vertical rock formations over 100 meters high and notable for their reddish hue.
